blocks:
Surah Sad, Makkan

The number of its verses is eighty-eight according to the Kufan count, and eighty-six according to the Hijazi, Basran, and Shamian counts. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) ص (1) Sad Its interpretation has preceded.

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): As for ** ص (Sad), it is a spring (عين) that flows from beneath the Throne (العرش). It is the one from which the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny) performed ablution when he was ascended (on the Mi'raj). Jibril enters it once every day, immerses himself in it, then comes out and shakes his wings. There is not a single drop that falls from his wings except that Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, creates from it an angel who glorifies Allah (يسبّح الله), sanctifies Him (يقدّسه), magnifies Him (يكبّره), and praises Him (يحمده) until the Day of Resurrection.

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) in the Hadith of the Ascension (المعراج): Then Allah revealed to me: "O Muhammad, draw near to Sad (صادٍ), wash your places of prostration (مساجدك), purify them, and pray to your Lord." So the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny) drew near to Sad, which is water flowing from the right leg (ساق) of the Throne (العرش)—the Hadith.

In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him) in a Hadith: He was asked, "What is Sad (صاد) from which he (meaning the Prophet, may Allah bless him and his progeny) was commanded to bathe when he was taken on the Night Journey (أسري به)?" He replied: "A spring (عين) that bursts forth from one of the corners (أركان) of the Throne (العرش), called the Water of Life (ماء الحياة). And that is what Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: ** ص وَالْقُرْآنِ ذِي الذِّكْرِ** (Sad, by the Quran containing the Reminder)."

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is one of the Names of Allah, the Exalted, by which He swore an oath.

** وَالْقُرْآنِ ذِي الذِّكْرِ (By the Quran containing the Reminder) This is sworn by, being conjoined (عطفاً) to Sad (صاد). Its answer (جوابه) is omitted, meaning: "Indeed, it is the truth (لحقّ)," which is indicated by His Almighty saying.

**(٢) بَلِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فِي عِزَّةٍ وَشِقاقٍ ** (2) Rather, the faithless are in [a state of] arrogance and defiance.

Meaning, that which the disbeliever disbelieved in was not due to a defect found in it. **بَلِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** are in arrogance concerning the truth and opposition to Allah and His Messenger, and for that reason, they disbelieved in Him.

And al-Qummi said that it is an oath (قَسَم) and its answer (جَوَاب) is بَلِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا . And this returns to what We have said.

(٣) كَمْ أَهْلَكْنا مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ مِنْ قَرْنٍ ** (3) How many generations have We destroyed before them! A warning for them on account of their disbelief in Him, due to arrogance and opposition (شقاق). ** فَنادَوْا ** Then they cried out, seeking help (استغاثة). ** وَلاتَ حِينَ مَناصٍ ** but it was not a time for escape. Meaning: the time is not a time of rescue (منجى) and escape (مفرّ). The tāʾ (التاء) was added to lā (لا) for emphasis (للتّأكيد).

(٤) وَعَجِبُوا أَنْ جاءَهُمْ مُنْذِرٌ مِنْهُمْ (4) And they were surprised that a warner should come to them from among themselves. a human being like them. وَقالَ الْكافِرُونَ ** and the faithless said, The explicit noun (الظاهر) was placed in the position of the pronoun (الضمير) in this verse, [done] out of anger towards them and condemnation of them, and to indicate that their disbelief emboldened them to make this statement. هذا ساحِرٌ ** 'This is a magician, regarding what he manifests as a miracle. **كَذَّابٌ ** a liar! regarding what he says concerning Allah.

(٥) أَجَعَلَ الْآلِهَةَ إِلهاً واحِداً إِنَّ هذا لَشَيْءٌ عُجابٌ ** (5) Has he reduced the gods to one God? This is indeed a strange thing! (It is) eloquent in strangeness, for it is contrary to what our fathers agreed upon. (٦) وَانْطَلَقَ الْمَلَأُ مِنْهُمْ أَنِ امْشُوا قائلين بعضهم لبعض امْشُوا وَاصْبِرُوا واثبتوا عَلى آلِهَتِكُمْ على عبادتها فلا ينفعكم مكالمته إِنَّ هذا لَشَيْءٌ يُرادُ قيل أي ان هذا لشيء من ريب الزمان يُرادُ بنا فلا مرد له وقيل ان هذا الذي يدعيه من الرِّياسة والترفع على العرب لشيء يريده كل أحد. (6) The elite among them went away, [saying,] 'Go and stand by your gods. This is indeed a thing that is intended. saying to one another: "Go and be firm عَلى آلِهَتِكُمْ ** (upon your gods)," upon their worship, for speaking to him will not benefit you. It was said: meaning, this is indeed a thing from the misfortunes (ريب الزمان) of time يُرادُ ** (intended) for us, and there is no repelling it. And it was said: this thing which he claims, of leadership (الرِّياسة) and superiority over the Arabs, is a thing that everyone desires.

(٧) ما سَمِعْنا بِهذا ** (regarding what he says) فِي الْمِلَّةِ الْآخِرَةِ ** (in the religion upon which we found our forefathers) **إِنْ هذا إِلَّا اخْتِلاقٌ ** (a lie that he fabricated). (7) We have not heard of this in the latter religion; this is nothing but a fabrication.

Al-Qummi said: It was revealed in Mecca when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم proclaimed the call (الدعوة) in Mecca. Quraysh gathered to Abu Talib عليه السلام and said: "O Abu Talib, your nephew has belittled our wisdom (أحلامنا), insulted our deities (آلِهتنا), corrupted our youth, and divided our community (جماعتنا). If what drives him to this is poverty (العُدم), we will gather wealth for him until he is the richest man in Quraysh, and we will make him our king." So Abu Talib informed the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and He said: "Even if they placed the sun in my right hand and the moon in my left, I would not abandon it. But let them give me one word (كلمة) by which they will rule the Arabs, and the non-Arabs (العجم) will submit to them, and they will be kings in Paradise (الجنّة)." So Abu Talib conveyed that to them, and they said: "Yes, and ten words (كلمات)." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to them: "They must testify that there is no god but Allah, and that I am the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم." They said: "Shall we abandon three hundred and sixty deities (آلهاً) and worship one God (إلهاً واحداً)?" Then Allah, the Glorified, revealed: بَلْ عَجِبُوا أَنْ جاءَهُمْ مُنْذِرٌ مِنْهُمْ (Rather, they are astonished that a warner has come to them from among themselves) up to His saying: إِلَّا اخْتِلاقٌ ** (meaning: confusion/muddle) أَأُنْزِلَ عَلَيْهِ الذِّكْرُ ** (Has the Reminder been sent down to him) up to His saying: مِنَ الْأَحْزابِ. (of the confederates.)

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام, he said: Abu Jahl ibn Hisham approached, accompanied by a group from Quraysh, and they entered upon Abu Talib and said: "Your nephew has harmed us and harmed our deities (آلهتنا). Call him and command him to cease [speaking ill of] our deities, and we will cease [speaking ill of] his God (إلهه)." He said: So Abu Talib sent for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and called Him. When the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم entered, He saw no one in the house except polytheists (مشركاً). So He said: "Peace be upon whoever follows the guidance (الهدى)," and then He sat down. Abu Talib informed Him of what they had come for. He said: "Do they not desire a word (كلمة) better for them than this, by which they will rule the Arabs and tread upon their necks?" Abu Jahl said: "Yes, and what is this word (الكلمة)?" He said: "You say: لا إله إلّا الله (There is no god but Allah)." He said: So they placed their fingers in their ears and rushed out fleeing (هُرّاباً), saying: ما سَمِعْنا بِهذا فِي الْمِلَّةِ الْآخِرَةِ إِنْ هذا إِلَّا اخْتِلاقٌ ** (We have not heard of this in the latter religion; this is nothing but a fabrication.) Then Allah revealed concerning their saying: ص وَالْقُرْآنِ ** (Ṣād. By the Quran) up to His saying: إِلَّا اخْتِلاقٌ. (but a fabrication.)

(٨) أَأُنْزِلَ عَلَيْهِ الذِّكْرُ مِنْ بَيْنِنا ** (8) Has the Reminder been sent down to him out of [all of] us? A denial of his being singled out for the revelation (الوحي), while he is like them, or inferior to them, in honor (الشرف) and leadership (الرياسة), due to their saying: لَوْ لا نُزِّلَ هذَا الْقُرْآنُ عَلى رَجُلٍ مِنَ الْقَرْيَتَيْنِ عَظِيمٍ (Why was not this Quran sent down to some great man from the two cities?) and examples such as this are proof that the basis of their denial was nothing but envy (الحسد) and the shortness of their sight concerning worldly debris (الحطام الدنيوي). بَلْ هُمْ فِي شَكٍّ مِنْ ذِكْرِي ** (8) Rather, they are in doubt about My Reminder. Concerning the Quran and the revelation (الوحي), due to their inclination towards blind imitation (التقليد) and their turning away from the proof (الدليل). **بَلْ لَمَّا يَذُوقُوا عَذابِ ** (8) Rather, they have not yet tasted My punishment. Rather, they have not yet tasted My punishment. So when they taste it, their doubt will vanish. And the meaning is that they will not believe in it until the punishment afflicts them, compelling them to affirm its truth.

(٩)** أَمْ عِنْدَهُمْ خَزائِنُ رَحْمَةِ رَبِّكَ الْعَزِيزِ الْوَهَّابِ ** (9) Do they possess the treasuries of the mercy of your Lord, the All-mighty, the All-munificent?

Rather, do they possess the treasuries of His mercy and are they under their disposal, so that they may bestow it upon whom they wish and withhold it from whom they wish, and thus choose some of their chiefs (صناديدهم) for prophethood? This means that prophethood is a gift (عطيّة) from Allah, which He bestows upon whom He wills among His servants, and there is no preventer for Him. For He is الْعَزِيزِ (the All-mighty), the Dominant (الغالب) Who is not overcome, الْوَهَّابِ (the All-munificent), Who has the right to grant everything He wills to whom He wills.

(١٠) أَمْ لَهُمْ مُلْكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَما بَيْنَهُما ** (10) Do they possess the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and what is between them? Or do they have access (مدخل) to this world (العالم) which is but a small part (جزء يسير) of His treasures (خزائنه)? فَلْيَرْتَقُوا فِي الْأَسْبابِ ** (Let them ascend through the means) meaning, if they possess that [dominion], then let them ascend the paths (المعارج) by which one reaches the Throne (العرش), until they settle upon it, manage the affair of the world, and send down the revelation (الوحي) to whomever they deem fit. And this is the ultimate mockery (غاية التّهكّم) against them. And it was said that what is meant by al-asbab (الأسباب) is the heavens (السماوات), because they are the causes (أسباب) of the lower (السّفليّة) events (الحوادث).

** (١١) جُنْدٌ ما هُنالِكَ مَهْزُومٌ مِنَ الْأَحْزابِ ** (A host of the confederates is there, which will be routed.) meaning, they are merely a host (جُنْدٌ ما) of the disbelievers who have formed confederacies (المتحزّبين) against the Messengers. Al-Qummi said: He means those who formed confederacies against you on the Day of the Trench (يوم الخندق). And it was said: مَهْزُومٌ ** (routed) means broken (مكسور) very soon (عمّا قريب). So, from where do they have the divine arrangements (التدابير الإلهية) and the authority (التصرف) over the Lordly affairs (الأمور الربّانية)? Or (it means): Do not be concerned (فلا تكترث) with what they say. And hunālika (هُنالِكَ) is a reference to the position (حيث وضعوا فيه أنفسهم) in which they placed themselves from the beginning (من الابتداء) of this saying.

(١٢) كَذَّبَتْ قَبْلَهُمْ قَوْمُ نُوحٍ وَعادٌ وَفِرْعَوْنُ ذُو الْأَوْتادِ (12) The people of Noah, and ‘Ad, and Pharaoh, the master of the stakes, denied [the truth] before them.

In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked concerning the Almighty's saying: وَفِرْعَوْنُ ذُو الْأَوْتادِ ** (and Pharaoh, the master of the stakes)—for what reason was he named dhū al-awtād (ذا الأوتاد)? He replied: Because when he tortured a man, he would lay him flat on the ground upon his face, and stretch out his hands and feet, and stake them down with four stakes (أوتاد) in the earth. And sometimes he would lay him flat upon a piece of wood (خشب) that was spread out, and stake his feet and hands with four stakes (أوتاد), then leave him in that state until he died. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, named him Pharaoh, dhū al-awtād (ذا الأوتاد).

And al-Qummi interpreted **الْأَوْتادِ ** (the stakes) as those [structures] by which he intended to ascend to the heaven.

(١٣) وَثَمُودُ وَقَوْمُ لُوطٍ وَأَصْحابُ الْأَيْكَةِ ** (13) and Thamūd, and the people of Lot, and the inhabitants of Aykah. And the inhabitants of al-Ghayḍah (الغيضة), who are the people of Shu'ayb. أُولئِكَ الْأَحْزابُ ** (Those are the confederates), meaning those who formed factions (المتحزّبين) against the Messengers, whom He made the defeated army. (١٤) إِنْ كُلٌّ إِلَّا كَذَّبَ الرُّسُلَ فَحَقَّ عِقابِ. (14) Not one of them but denied the apostles, so My retribution became due. (١٥) وَما يَنْظُرُ هؤُلاءِ ** (15) These do not await And what your people, or all the confederates (الأحزاب), await إِلَّا صَيْحَةً واحِدَةً ** (but a single Cry), which is the blast (النفخة). ما لَها مِنْ فَواقٍ ** (for which there will be no respite). It was said: meaning, no pause (توقّف) for the duration of a fawāq (فَواقٍ), which is the time between two milkings, or a return and repetition, for in it the milk returns to the udder. And al-Qummi said: meaning, they will not recover (لا يفيقون) from the punishment. And it was read with the ḍammah (ضمّ) on the fā’ (الفاء), and both are linguistic variants (لغتان).

(١٦) وَقالُوا رَبَّنا عَجِّلْ لَنا قِطَّنا

(16) And they said, 'Our Lord! Hasten for us our share [of the punishment]

our portion (قسطنا) of the punishment which He promised us with. In al-Ma'ani, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) regarding its meaning, He said: "their portion (نصيبهم) of the punishment **قَبْلَ يَوْمِ الْحِسابِ ** before the Day of Reckoning.'

They hastened that out of mockery.

(17) اصْبِرْ عَلى ما يَقُولُونَ وَاذْكُرْ عَبْدَنا داوُدَ ذَا الْأَيْدِ (17) Be patient with what they say, and remember Our servant David, the man of strength. In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The hand (الْيَد) in the speech of the Arabs means strength (القوّة) and favor (النّعمة). Then He recited this Ayah. إِنَّهُ أَوَّابٌ (He was) indeed a frequent penitent. It was said, meaning: frequently returning (رجّاع) to the pleasure (مرضاة) of Allah due to his strength (قوّته) in the religion (الدين). And al-Qummi (said it means): frequently supplicating (دعّاء). It was said that he used to fast one day and break his fast one day, and he would stand in prayer (يقوم) half the night.

(١٨) إِنَّا سَخَّرْنَا الْجِبالَ مَعَهُ يُسَبِّحْنَ ** (Indeed We subjected the mountains [to him], glorifying [Him] with him) Its interpretation has already preceded in سورة الأنبياء and سبا. بِالْعَشِيِّ وَالْإِشْراقِ ** (at evening and sunrise) when the sun rises, meaning it illuminates and its rays become clear (يصفو شعاعها). (١٩) وَالطَّيْرَ مَحْشُورَةً ** (and the birds, gathered) to him from every direction (من كلّ جانب). كُلٌّ لَهُ أَوَّابٌ ** (all were penitent to Him) meaning every one of the mountains and the birds, for the sake of his glorification (تسبيحه), was returning (رجّاع) to glorification (التسبيح). (٢٠) وَشَدَدْنا مُلْكَهُ ** (And We consolidated his kingdom) and We strengthened it with awe (الهيبة), victory (النصرة), and the multitude of soldiers (كثرة الجنود). وَآتَيْناهُ الْحِكْمَةَ وَفَصْلَ الْخِطابِ ** (and We gave him wisdom and conclusive speech.) It was said that it is the separation of disputes (فصل الخصام) whereby the truth (الحقّ) is distinguished from falsehood (الباطل). And it was said that it is the distinct speech (الكلام المفصول) which is not confusing to the listener. In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): that it is the knowledge of languages (معرفة اللّغات). In al-Jawami', narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): it is His saying: "The evidence (البيّنة) is upon the claimant (المدّعي) and the oath (اليمين) is upon the defendant (المدّعى عليه)." And many reports (اخبار كثيرة) have been narrated that our Imams (peace be upon them) were granted الْحِكْمَةَ وَفَصْلَ الْخِطابِ. (wisdom and conclusive speech.)

(٢١) وَهَلْ أَتاكَ نَبَأُ الْخَصْمِ ** (Has the story of the litigants come to you) In this is an expression of wonder (تعجيب) and an enticement (تشويق) to listen to it. ** إِذْ تَسَوَّرُوا الْمِحْرابَ ** (when they scaled the wall of the sanctuary) when they climbed the wall (سور) of the chamber (الغرفة). (٢٢) إِذْ دَخَلُوا عَلى داوُدَ فَفَزِعَ مِنْهُمْ ** (when they entered into David’s presence, and he was alarmed by them.) because they descended upon him from above, and it was a day of seclusion (الاحتجاب) while the guards were at the door. ** قالُوا لا تَخَفْ خَصْمانِ بَغى بَعْضُنا عَلى بَعْضٍ فَاحْكُمْ بَيْنَنا بِالْحَقِّ وَلا تُشْطِطْ ** (They said, ‘Do not be afraid. We are two litigants, one of whom has wronged the other. Judge justly between us, and do not be unfair,) and do not deviate/be unjust (تَجُرْ) in the judgment (الحكومة). ** وَاهْدِنا إِلى سَواءِ الصِّراطِ ** (and guide us to the right path.’) to its middle (وسطه), which is justice (العدل).

** إِنَّ هذا أَخِي لَهُ تِسْعٌ وَتِسْعُونَ نَعْجَةً وَلِيَ نَعْجَةٌ واحِدَةٌ ** (23) Indeed this is my brother. He has ninety-nine ewes, and I have a single ewe. It is the female of the sheep (الضّأن), and it may be used metaphorically (يُكَنَّى) for a woman (المرأة). فَقالَ أَكْفِلْنِيها ** (Then he said, 'Entrust it to me') [Meaning:] Make me its owner (مَلَّكَنِيهَا). Its origin is: "And make me responsible for it," or "Make it my share (كِفْلِي)," meaning, my portion (نَصِيبِي). وَعَزَّنِي فِي الْخِطابِ ** (and he prevailed over me in the argument) And he overcame me (غَلَبَنِي) in his addressing of me.

(٢٤) قالَ لَقَدْ ظَلَمَكَ بِسُؤالِ نَعْجَتِكَ إِلى نِعاجِهِ وَإِنَّ كَثِيراً مِنَ الْخُلَطاءِ ** (He said, 'He has certainly wronged you by asking for your ewe to add to his ewes, and indeed many partners) The partners (الشركاء) who mixed their wealth (أموالهم), the plural (جمع) of خليط, ** لَيَبْغِي ** certainly transgresses (ليتعدّى) بَعْضُهُمْ عَلى بَعْضٍ ** إِلَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ وَقَلِيلٌ ما هُمْ ** (one another, except those who have faith and do righteous deeds, and few are they!) And they are few. The ما is added (مزيدة) for ambiguity (للإبهام) and astonishment (والتعجّب) at their scarcity (قلّتهم). ** وَظَنَّ داوُدُ أَنَّما فَتَنَّاهُ ** (And David realized that We had tested him.) We tested him (امتحنّاه) with that judgment (الحكومة): did he become aware (تنبّه) through it? ** فَاسْتَغْفَرَ رَبَّهُ وَخَرَّ راكِعاً ساجِداً وَأَنابَ ** (So he sought forgiveness from his Lord, and fell down bowing and prostrating, and repented.) And returned (ورجع) to Allah with repentance (بالتوبة).

(25) فَغَفَرْنا لَهُ ذلِكَ ** (So We forgave him that) meaning, that for which he sought forgiveness. وَإِنَّ لَهُ عِنْدَنا لَزُلْفى ** (and indeed he has a near station with Us) a nearness after the forgiveness, وَحُسْنَ مَآبٍ ** (and a good retreat) a return in Paradise. (26) يا داوُدُ إِنَّا جَعَلْناكَ خَلِيفَةً فِي الْأَرْضِ فَاحْكُمْ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ بِالْحَقِّ وَلا تَتَّبِعِ الْهَوى فَيُضِلَّكَ عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَضِلُّونَ عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ لَهُمْ عَذابٌ شَدِيدٌ بِما نَسُوا يَوْمَ الْحِسابِ ** (O David! Indeed, We have made you a vicegerent in the earth. So judge between the people with justice, and do not follow [your] desires, lest they should lead you astray from the way of Allah. Indeed, those who stray from the way of Allah, there is a severe punishment for them because they forgot the Day of Reckoning.) A discussion regarding the vicegerency (خلافة) of David (peace be upon him) has already preceded in Surah Luqman.

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him) in the tradition concerning the infallibility (عصمة) of the Prophets, he said: "As for David, what do those before you say concerning him?" It was said: "They say that David (peace be upon him) was praying in his sanctuary (محراب) when Iblis appeared to him in the form of a bird, the most beautiful of birds. David (peace be upon him) cut short his prayer and stood up to catch the bird. The bird flew out to the courtyard, so he went out following it. The bird flew to the roof, so he climbed the roof in pursuit of it. The bird then fell into the house of Uriah ibn Hayyan. David (peace be upon him) looked down following the bird and saw Uriah's wife bathing. When he looked at her, he desired her. He had previously sent Uriah out on one of his campaigns, so he wrote to his commander: 'Put Uriah forward before the Ark (التابوت).' So he was put forward, and Uriah was victorious over the polytheists. This was difficult for David (peace be upon him), so he wrote to him a second time: 'Put him forward before the Ark (التابوت).' So he was put forward, and Uriah was killed. Then David (peace be upon him) married his wife." He (al-Rida, peace be upon him) said: Then al-Rida (peace be upon him) struck his hand upon his forehead and said: "Indeed, we belong to Allah, and indeed, to Him shall we return (إنّا لله وإنّا إليه راجعون). You have attributed to a Prophet among the Prophets of Allah negligence in his prayer, such that he went out following a bird, and then [attributed to him] immorality (الفاحشة), and then murder!" It was said: "O son of the Messenger of Allah, what then was his mistake (خطيئته)?" He said: "Woe unto you! David (peace be upon him) only thought that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, had not created any creature more knowledgeable than him. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent the two angels to him, and تَسَوَّرُوا الْمِحْرابَ ** (they scaled the sanctuary). They said to him: خَصْمانِ بَغى بَعْضُنا عَلى بَعْضٍ فَاحْكُمْ بَيْنَنا بِالْحَقِّ وَلا تُشْطِطْ وَاهْدِنا إِلى سَواءِ الصِّراطِ إِنَّ هذا أَخِي لَهُ تِسْعٌ وَتِسْعُونَ نَعْجَةً وَلِيَ نَعْجَةٌ واحِدَةٌ فَقالَ أَكْفِلْنِيها وَعَزَّنِي فِي الْخِطابِ ** (Two litigants: one of us has wronged the other. So judge between us with justice, and do not be unjust, and guide us to the right path. Indeed, this is my brother; he has ninety-nine ewes, and I have a single ewe. He said, 'Entrust it to me,' and he prevailed over me in the discourse.) David (peace be upon him) hastened against the defendant and said: لَقَدْ ظَلَمَكَ بِسُؤالِ نَعْجَتِكَ إِلى نِعاجِهِ ** (He has certainly wronged you by demanding your ewe to be added to his ewes.) He did not ask the plaintiff for evidence (البيّنة) regarding that, nor did he turn to the defendant to ask him what he had to say. This was his mistake (خطيئته): a procedural error in judgment (رسم حكم), not what you have assumed. Do you not hear Allah the Almighty saying: يا داوُدُ إِنَّا جَعَلْناكَ خَلِيفَةً فِي الْأَرْضِ فَاحْكُمْ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ بِالْحَقِ ** to the end of the Ayah?" It was said: "O son of the Messenger of Allah, what then is his story with Uriah?" Al-Rida (peace be upon him) said: "During the days of David (peace be upon him), if a woman's husband died or was killed, she would never marry after him. The first person for whom Allah the Almighty permitted marriage to the wife of a man who was killed was David (peace be upon him). So he married Uriah's wife after he was killed and her waiting period (عدّتها) had ended. That is what became difficult for the people because of Uriah."

And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): [a narration] close to what the general populace (العامّة) narrated, which al-Rida (peace be upon him) denied, as has passed, with additions, and it contains what it contains. And from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding His saying: وَظَنَّ داوُدُ (David thought) (peace be upon him), meaning: he knew (علم). وَأَنابَ ** (and he turned [to Allah]), meaning: he repented (تاب). And it is mentioned that David (peace be upon him) wrote to his commander not to put Uriah forward before the Ark (التابوت) and to send him back, but Uriah was sent to his family, stayed eight days, and then died.

And in al-Majalis, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "Indeed, the satisfaction of the people cannot be possessed, and their tongues cannot be controlled. Did they not attribute to David (peace be upon him) that he followed the bird until he looked at Uriah's wife and desired her, and that he put her husband forward before the Ark (التابوت) until he was killed, and then married her?"

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He said: "I shall not be brought a man who claims that David (peace be upon him) married Uriah's wife except that I will flog him two punishments: one for [slandering] prophethood (النبوّة) and one for [slandering] Islam (الإسلام)." And it is narrated that he said: "Whoever narrates the story of David (peace be upon him) according to what the storytellers (القصّاص) relate, I shall flog him one hundred and sixty [lashes]."

(٢٧) وَما خَلَقْنَا السَّماءَ وَالْأَرْضَ وَما بَيْنَهُما باطِلاً ** (27) And We did not create the heaven and the earth and what is between them in vain. without wisdom (لا حكمة فيه). ذلِكَ ظَنُّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فَوَيْلٌ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنَ النَّارِ ** That is the assumption of the faithless. So woe to the faithless because of the Fire! because of this assumption. (٢٨) أَمْ نَجْعَلُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ كَالْمُفْسِدِينَ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (28) Shall We treat those who have faith and do righteous deeds like those who cause corruption in the earth? This is a denial of equality. أَمْ نَجْعَلُ الْمُتَّقِينَ كَالْفُجَّارِ ** Shall We treat the Godwary like the vicious? It is said that He first denied equality between the believers and the disbelievers, and then between the Godwary among the believers and the criminals among them. It is also possible that this is a repetition of the first denial, considering two other attributes that prevent equality from the All-Wise, the All-Merciful (الحكيم الرحيم).

And in al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), that He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ ** (Those who have faith and do righteous deeds) are the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) and his companions. كَالْمُفْسِدِينَ فِي الْأَرْضِ (like those who cause corruption in the earth) He said: Ḥabtar (حبتر) and Zayq (زيق) and their companions. أَمْ نَجْعَلُ الْمُتَّقِينَ ** (Shall We treat the Godwary) is the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين). كَالْفُجَّارِ (like the vicious) are Ḥabtar (حبتر) and Zalām (زلام) and their companions. And these terms (الألفاظ) are allusions (كنايات) to the three (individuals).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: "It is not proper for the people of truth (أهل الحقّ) to lower themselves to the status of the people of falsehood (أهل الباطل), because Allah has not placed the people of truth in the same status as the people of falsehood in His sight. Have they not recognized the meaning of Allah's saying in His Book when He says: **أَمْ نَجْعَلُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** (Shall We treat those who have faith)... (the rest of the Ayah)?"

In al-Khisal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "Indeed, the people of piety (أهل التقوى) have signs (علامات) by which they are known: truthfulness in speech (صدق الحديث), fulfilling the trust (أداء الأمانة), keeping the covenant (الوفاء بالعهد), little boasting (قلّة الفخر), endurance (التحمّل), maintaining kinship ties (صلة الأرحام), mercy towards the weak (رحمة الضّعفاء), little compliance with women (قلّة المواتاة للنّساء), expenditure of good deeds (بذل المعروف), good character (حسن الخلق), broad forbearance (سعة الحلم), and following knowledge in that which brings one closer to Allah, the Exalted (الله تعالى)." And in another narration from Him (peace be upon him), He said: "The vicious person (الفاجر): if you entrust him, he betrays you; if you accompany him, he disgraces you; and if you trust him, he does not advise you sincerely."

**(٢٩) كِتابٌ أَنْزَلْناهُ إِلَيْكَ مُبارَكٌ لِيَدَّبَّرُوا آياتِهِ وَلِيَتَذَكَّرَ أُولُوا الْأَلْبابِ ** (29) A Book that We have sent down to you, blessed, that they may contemplate its signs, and that those who possess intellect may take admonition.

[The word مُبارَكٌ means] highly beneficial (نفّاع). [The word الْأَلْبابِ means] penetrating (الثاقبة).

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding that they may contemplate its signs: [It refers to] the Commander of the Faithful and the Imams (peace be upon them), for they are those who possess intellect. He said: And the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) used to boast about it and say: "No one before me nor after me has been given the like of what I have been given."

(٣٠) وَوَهَبْنا لِداوُدَ سُلَيْمانَ نِعْمَ الْعَبْدُ ** (30) And We gave Solomon to David—an excellent servant [was he]! أي نِعْمَ الْعَبْدُ سليمان إِنَّهُ أَوَّابٌ كثير الرجوع إلى الله بالتوبة والذكر. i.e., نِعْمَ الْعَبْدُ ** (an excellent servant [was he]) is Sulayman. إِنَّهُ أَوَّابٌ ** (Indeed, he was frequent penitent) means: frequent in returning to Allah with repentance (التوبة) and remembrance (الذكر). (٣١) إِذْ عُرِضَ عَلَيْهِ بِالْعَشِيِ الصَّافِناتُ الْجِيادُ ** (31) When there were presented to him in the evening the standing, swift steeds, بعد الظهر الصَّافِناتُ الْجِيادُ الصافن الخيل الذي يقوم على طرف سنبك يد أو رجل وهو من الصفات المحمودة في الخيل والْجِيادُ قيل جمع جواد أو جود وهو الذي يسرع في جريه وقيل الذي يجود بالركض وقيل جمع جيّد. after noon (الظهر). الصَّافِناتُ الْجِيادُ ** (al-ṣāfināt al-jiyād): الصافن (al-ṣāfin) is the horse (الخيل) that stands upon the tip of the hoof (سنبك) of one foreleg (يد) or hindleg (رجل), and this is one of the praiseworthy characteristics (الصفات المحمودة) in horses. وَالْجِيادُ (al-jiyād): It was said that it is the plural of جواد (jawād) or جود (jūd), which is that which is swift in its running (جريه). And it was said that it is that which is generous (يجود) in galloping (الركض). And it was said that it is the plural of جيّد (jayyid).

(٣٢)** فَقالَ إِنِّي أَحْبَبْتُ حُبَّ الْخَيْرِ عَنْ ذِكْرِ رَبِّي ** (32) He said, 'Indeed I have preferred the love of [worldly] good to the remembrance of my Lord'

It was said that the origin of أَحْبَبْتُ ** is that it should be transitive using (على) because it is in the meaning of (آثرت) (I preferred), but when it was substituted in place of (أَنَابَ) (he turned/returned), its transitivity was made using (عن). And it was said that it is in the meaning of (تَقَاعَدْتُ) (I held back). And حُبَّ الْخَيْرِ ** is the object for the sake of which the action was done (مَفْعُولٌ لَهُ). And الْخَيْرِ ** means abundant wealth (المال الكثير). What is meant by it here is the horses (الخيل) which distracted him from remembrance (الذكر). And in the Hadith: Goodness (الخير) is tied to the forelocks (بنواصيها) of horses (الخيل). حَتَّى تَوارَتْ بِالْحِجابِ ** (until it was concealed by the veil) means the sun set (غربت الشمس). He likened its setting to the concealment (تَوَارِي) of a hidden thing (المخبأة) by its veil (حجابها), and its omission (referring to the sun) without mention is due to the indication of the evening (العشيّ) upon it.

(٣٣) رُدُّوها عَلَيَ ** (33) 'Bring them back to me!' The pronoun refers to the sun (الشمس). فَطَفِقَ مَسْحاً ** (So he began to stroke) meaning, he began to stroke, بِالسُّوقِ وَالْأَعْناقِ. (the shanks and the necks.)

In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "Indeed, Sulayman son of Dawud (peace be upon him) was shown the horses one day in the late afternoon (بالعشيّ). He became preoccupied with looking at them until the sun disappeared behind the veil (بالحجاب). So he said to the angels: 'Bring the sun back to me (رُدُّوا الشَّمْسَ عَلَيَّ) so that I may pray my prayer in its time.' So they brought it back. Then he stood up and wiped his shanks (ساقيه) and his neck (عنقه), and commanded his companions who had missed the prayer with him to do the same. That was their ablution (وضوءهم) for the prayer. Then he stood and prayed. When he finished, the sun set and the stars appeared. That is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَوَهَبْنا لِداوُدَ سُلَيْمانَ ** (And We gave Sulayman to Dawud) up to His saying وَالْأَعْناقِ** (and the necks)."

In al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "Indeed, these horses preoccupied him from the 'Asr prayer until its time elapsed." He said: "And in the narrations of our companions (أصحابنا), it is stated that he missed the beginning of the time (أوّل الوقت)."

In al-Kafi and al-Faqih, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: إِنَّ الصَّلاةَ كانَتْ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ كِتاباً مَوْقُوتاً (Indeed, the prayer is a written ordinance for the faithful, [to be performed] at [their] appointed times). He said: "It means obligatory (مفروضاً), and it does not mean the time of its lapse (وقت فوتها). If that time passes and he then prays it, this prayer of his would not be considered performed (مؤدّاة). If that were the case, Sulayman son of Dawud (peace be upon him) would have perished when he prayed it outside its time. Rather, whenever he remembers it, he prays it."

In al-Ilal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): [A narration] close to it.

In al-Majma', Ibn 'Abbas said: "I asked 'Ali (peace be upon him) about this Ayah. He said: 'What has reached you concerning it, O son of 'Abbas?' I said: 'Yes, I heard Ka'b say that Sulayman was preoccupied with the display of the horses until the prayer escaped him. So he said: رُدُّوها عَلَيَ ** (Bring them back to me), meaning the horses, and there were fourteen of them. So he commanded that their shanks (سوقها) and necks (أعناقها) be struck with the sword, killing them. So Allah stripped him of his kingdom for fourteen days because he wronged the horses by killing them.' 'Ali (peace be upon him) said: 'Ka'b lied. Rather, Sulayman was preoccupied with the display of the horses one day because he intended to wage war (جهاد) against the enemy, حَتَّى تَوارَتْ ** (until it disappeared)—meaning the sun—بِالْحِجابِ ** (behind the veil). So he said, commanding the angels entrusted with the sun: رُدُّوها عَلَيَ ** (Bring it back to me). So it was brought back, and he prayed the 'Asr prayer in its time. Indeed, the Prophets of Allah do not commit injustice (يظلمون) nor do they command injustice (الظلم), because they are infallible (معصومون), purified (مطهّرون).'"

And al-Qummi mentioned something close to what Ka'b said, then he narrated the story of his ring (خاتمه) from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that it was lost from him for forty days due to his killing of the horses, and a devil (شيطان) stole it and sat in his place during that period, until the end of what he mentioned, which is inappropriate for the Prophets, unless it is symbolic (مرموزاً) and something else is intended by it, just as its like preceded in the story of Harut and Marut.

(٣٤) وَلَقَدْ فَتَنَّا سُلَيْمانَ وَأَلْقَيْنا عَلى كُرْسِيِّهِ جَسَداً ثُمَّ أَنابَ. (34) Certainly We tried Solomon, and cast a body on his throne, whereafter he turned penitently [to Us].

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: Solomon said one day in his gathering: "I shall certainly go around tonight to seventy women, so that every woman among them gives birth to a boy who will strike with the sword in the way of Allah." But he did not say إن شاء الله (if Allah wills). So he went around to them, but none of them conceived except one woman, who came with half a child (بشقّ ولد). He (the narrator/Prophet) said, then he (the Prophet) said: "By Him in Whose Hand is the soul of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, had he said إن شاء الله, they would certainly have fought in the way of Allah as horsemen." And the body (الجسد) that was **عَلى كُرْسِيِّهِ ** (on his throne) was this (half-child).

And from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: When a son was born to Solomon عليه السلام, the Jinn and the devils said to one another: "If a child of his lives, we shall certainly meet from him the same affliction (البلاء) we met from his father." So he feared for him from them, and he had him nursed in al-Muzn, which is السحاب (the clouds). He did not realize until it was placed عَلى كُرْسِيِّهِ ** (on his throne) dead. This was an admonition (تنبيها) that caution (الحذر) is of no avail against destiny (القدر), and He was only censured for his fear of the devils.

And it was said that the body (الجسد) was that devil who had sat in his place upon his throne. He was named the body without a soul (الجسد الذي لا روح فيه) because he was represented in a form that was not truly so (i.e., a mere physical manifestation). And this is the saying of the general populace (العامّة) who narrate that story in which the ring (الخاتم) is mentioned. Except that they mentioned regarding the reason for his trial concerning his dominion (ملكه) that his wife used to worship an idol (صورة) in his house for forty days, and he was unaware of that.

(٣٥) قالَ رَبِّ اغْفِرْ لِي وَهَبْ لِي مُلْكاً لا يَنْبَغِي لِأَحَدٍ مِنْ بَعْدِي إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْوَهَّابُ. (35) He said, 'My Lord! Forgive me, and grant me a kingdom that will not belong to anyone after me. Indeed, You are the All-munificent.' (٣٦) فَسَخَّرْنا لَهُ الرِّيحَ ** meaning, We made it subservient to his obedience, in response to his supplication تَجْرِي بِأَمْرِهِ رُخاءً ** soft (ليّنة), not shaking/disturbing (لا تزعزع) حَيْثُ أَصابَ ** meaning, he willed (أراد). (٣٧) وَالشَّياطِينَ كُلَّ بَنَّاءٍ وَغَوَّاصٍ. (37) and the devils, every builder and diver, (٣٨) وَآخَرِينَ مُقَرَّنِينَ فِي الْأَصْفادِ ** some of them were bound together with others in chains (السلاسل) so that they might refrain from evil—thus it was said. And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) (said) they are those who disobeyed Sulayman when Allah stripped him of his kingdom. And some of this story has already preceded in Surat Saba’ (سورة سبأ). (٣٩) هذا عَطاؤُنا ** meaning, this which We have given you of sovereignty (الملك), abundance (البسطة), and authority (التسلط) over that which no one else besides you has been given authority over عَطاؤُنا فَامْنُنْ أَوْ أَمْسِكْ ** so give to whomever you will and withhold from whomever you will بِغَيْرِ حِسابٍ without being held accountable for your bestowal or withholding, due to the delegation of the disposal thereof to you.

** وَإِنَّ لَهُ عِنْدَنا لَزُلْفى ** (40) and indeed he has a proximity to Us in the Hereafter, along with the great dominion he possessed in the world, وَحُسْنَ مَآبٍ (and a good destination), which is Paradise.

In al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him): He was asked: "Is it permissible for a Prophet of Allah to be stingy (بخيلاً)?" He replied: "No." It was said: "Then what is the interpretation (وجهه) and meaning of Sulayman's (peace be upon him) saying: رَبِّ اغْفِرْ لِي وَهَبْ لِي مُلْكاً لا يَنْبَغِي لِأَحَدٍ مِنْ بَعْدِي ** (35) 'My Lord! Forgive me, and grant me a kingdom that will not belong to anyone after me.' He said: "Dominion (الملك) is of two types: a dominion taken by conquest (بالغلبة), injustice (الجور), and coercion of people (إجبار الناس); and a dominion taken from Allah, exalted be His remembrance, like the dominion of the family of Ibrahim, and the dominion of Talut and Dhul Qarnayn. So, Sulayman said: هَبْ لِي مُلْكاً لا يَنْبَغِي لِأَحَدٍ مِنْ بَعْدِي ** (Grant me a kingdom that will not belong to anyone after me) meaning that it should not be said that it was taken by conquest, injustice, and coercion of people. So, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, subjugated for him: لَهُ الرِّيحَ تَجْرِي بِأَمْرِهِ رُخاءً حَيْثُ أَصابَ ** (36) the wind, blowing gently at his command wherever he desired, and made its morning journey a month and its evening journey a month. And Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, subjugated for him: الشَّياطِينَ كُلَّ بَنَّاءٍ وَغَوَّاصٍ ** (37) the devils, every builder and diver, and taught him the speech of the birds (منطق الطير) and established him firmly on the earth. Thus, people knew, both in his time and after it, that his dominion did not resemble the dominion of the tyrannical kings (الملوك الجبّارين) among men, nor those who ruled by conquest and injustice." It was asked: "Then what about the saying of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: 'May Allah have mercy on my brother Sulayman ibn Dawud (peace be upon him), how un-stingy (ما كان أبخله) he was'?" He said: "His saying has two interpretations (وجهان): The first is: How un-stingy he was with his honor (بعرضه) and with allowing ill-speech concerning him. The second interpretation is: He means, 'How un-stingy he was,' if he (Sulayman) had intended what the ignorant people (الجهّال) assume."

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding the Almighty's saying: هذا عَطاؤُنا ** (39) 'This is Our gift,' the Ayah. He said: "Sulayman (peace be upon him) was given a great dominion. Then this Ayah applied to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, so it was His right to give whom He willed and what He willed, and to withhold from whom He willed what He willed. And He gave him (the Prophet) better than what He gave Sulayman (peace be upon him), due to His saying: ما آتاكُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَما نَهاكُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا.** (7) Take whatever the Apostle gives you, and refrain from whatever he forbids you.

And narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): It was said to him: "Is it incumbent upon us (حقّاً علينا) to ask you?" He said: "Yes." It was said: "Is it incumbent upon you (حقّاً عليكم) to answer us?" He said: "No. That is up to us (إلينا). If We will, We do so, and if We will, We do not. Do you not hear the Almighty's saying: هذا عَطاؤُنا فَامْنُنْ أَوْ أَمْسِكْ بِغَيْرِ حِسابٍ. (39) 'This is Our gift, so give or withhold without reckoning.'"

(٤١) وَاذْكُرْ عَبْدَنا أَيُّوبَ إِذْ نادى رَبَّهُ أَنِّي مَسَّنِيَ الشَّيْطانُ بِنُصْبٍ ** (41) And remember Our servant Job, when he called out to his Lord, [saying,] 'The devil has afflicted me with distress and torment.' The word بِنُصْبٍ means fatigue (بِتَعَبٍ). It was also recited with a fatḥah on the nūn and with two fatḥahs. وَعَذابٍ ** means pain (أَلَمٌ). This is a narration (حِكَايَةٌ) of his speech. (٤٢) ارْكُضْ بِرِجْلِكَ ** (42) 'Stamp your foot [on the ground]: this is a cool bath and a drink.' This is a narration (حِكَايَةٌ) of what He responded with, meaning: strike the ground with your foot. هذا مُغْتَسَلٌ بارِدٌ وَشَرابٌ ** So he struck it, and a spring gushed forth. Then it was said: هذا مُغْتَسَلٌ , meaning: you wash yourself with it and drink from it, and it will heal your inner and outer self. (٤٣)** وَوَهَبْنا لَهُ أَهْلَهُ وَمِثْلَهُمْ مَعَهُمْ ** (43) And We gave him his family and the like of them along with them, By resurrecting them after their death.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked how he was given مِثْلَهُمْ مَعَهُمْ ** (the like of them along with them). He said: "He resurrected for him those of his children who had died before that, according to their appointed times (بِآجَالِهِمْ), similar to those who perished on that day."

And in al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: Allah resurrected لَهُ أَهْلَهُ ** (his family) who were there before the affliction, and He resurrected for him those who died while he was undergoing the affliction. رَحْمَةً مِنَّا وَذِكْرى لِأُولِي الْأَلْبابِ ** (as a mercy from Us and a reminder for those who possess intellect) so that they may await relief (الْفَرَجَ) through patience and seeking refuge (اللُّجَأ) in Allah concerning what afflicts them.

(٤٤) وَخُذْ بِيَدِكَ ضِغْثاً ** (44) And take in your hand a bundle of rushes, A small bundle of wood. فَاضْرِبْ بِهِ وَلا تَحْنَثْ and strike with it, and do not break your oath. And that was because he swore to strike his wife concerning an matter, then he regretted it. So Allah dissolved His oath by that (action), and it is a remaining dispensation (رخصة باقية) in the prescribed punishments (الحدود), as has been narrated from them (peace be upon them).

إِنَّا وَجَدْناهُ صابِراً ** (Indeed We found him patient) in what afflicted him concerning his soul, his family, and his wealth. نِعْمَ الْعَبْدُ أيّوب (ع) (How excellent a servant was Job (peace be upon him)). إِنَّهُ أَوَّابٌ ** (Indeed he was penitent) turning towards Allah with all his being (بشراشره).

In al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The affliction (بليّة) of Ayyub (peace be upon him) with which He afflicted him in this world was only due to a blessing (نعمة) with which Allah favored him, and he rendered thanks for it. Iblis, at that time, was not veiled from the Throne (العرش). When the deed of Ayyub (peace be upon him) ascended—his rendering thanks for the blessing—Iblis envied him and said: "O Lord, Ayyub (peace be upon him) only rendered thanks for this blessing because of what You gave him. If You were to intervene between him and his worldly possessions (دنياه), he would not render thanks for the blessing to You. So, grant me authority over his worldly possessions so that You may know that he will not render thanks for the blessing." He (Allah) said: "I have granted you authority over his worldly possessions." So, he left him neither worldly possessions nor children without destroying all of it, while he (Ayyub) was praising Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. Then he (Iblis) returned to Him and said: "O Lord, Ayyub knows that You will restore to him his worldly possessions that You took from him. So, grant me authority over his body, and You will know that he will not render thanks for the blessing." The Mighty and Majestic said: "I have granted you authority over his body, except for his eyes, his heart, his tongue, and his hearing." He (Iblis) said: So he swooped down quickly, fearing that the Mercy of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, might overtake him and intervene between him and it. Then he blew into his nostrils from the fire of al-Samum (نار السموم), and his body became spots upon spots.

And narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him) is a similar account, with the addition: When the affliction intensified upon him, and he was at the end of his trial, his companions came and said: "O Ayyub, we do not know anyone afflicted with a trial like this except due to an evil secret (سريرة شرّ). Perhaps you concealed some evil in what you show us." He said: At that point, Ayyub (peace be upon him) supplicated secretly to his Lord, the Mighty and Majestic, and said: "O Lord, You have afflicted me with this trial, and You know that never were two matters presented to me except that I committed myself to the harsher of the two upon my body, and I never ate a meal except that there was an orphan on my dining cloth (خواني). If I had the position of an opponent (مقعد الخصم) before You, I would present my argument (حجّتي)." He said: Then a cloud appeared to him, and a speaker spoke within it, saying: "O Ayyub, present your argument!" He said: So he tightened his waist wrapper (ميزره) and knelt on his knees, and said: "You afflicted me with this trial, and You know that never were two matters presented to me except that I committed myself to the harsher of the two upon my body, and I never ate a meal of food except that there was an orphan on my dining cloth." He said: Then it was said to him: "O Ayyub, who made obedience beloved to you?" He said: So he took a handful of dust, placed it in his mouth, and then said: "You, O Lord."

And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, afflicted Ayyub (peace be upon him) without a sin (بلا ذنب), and he was patient until he was reproached (عيّر), and indeed the Prophets do not endure reproach (التعيير).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah the Almighty afflicts the believer with every trial and causes him to die every kind of death, but He does not afflict him with the loss of his intellect (عقله). Do you not see how Iblis was granted authority over Ayyub's (peace be upon him) wealth, his family, and everything belonging to him, but was not granted authority over his intellect? He left him (his intellect) so that he could affirm the Oneness of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. And in a narration: So he was granted authority over Ayyub (peace be upon him) and disfigured his appearance (خلقه), but was not granted authority over his religion (دينه).

And in al-Khisal and al-Ilal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Ayyub was afflicted for seven years without a sin (بلا ذنب).

And in al-Khisal, narrated from him, from his father (peace be upon them both), he said: Indeed, Ayyub (peace be upon him) was afflicted without a sin for seven years, and indeed the Prophets are infallible (معصومون); they do not sin, nor do they deviate, nor do they commit a sin, neither small nor great. And he said: Indeed, Ayyub, despite all that he was afflicted with, neither did his scent become foul, nor did his appearance become ugly, nor did any thick pus (مِدَّة) (1) of blood or discharge come out of him, nor did anyone who saw him find him repulsive, nor did anyone who witnessed him feel alienated by him, nor did any part of his body become infested with worms (تدوّد). And thus does Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, deal with all those whom He afflicts among His Prophets and His honored friends (أوليائه المكرّمين). The people only shunned him due to his poverty and weakness in his apparent state, because of their ignorance of the support (التأييد) and relief (الفرج) that he had with his Lord, exalted be His remembrance. And the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: "The people most severely afflicted are the Prophets, then the friends (of Allah), then the best of people, and then the next best." Allah only afflicted him with the great trial—which makes all people seem insignificant alongside it—so that they would not claim divinity (الربوبيّة) for him when they witnessed the great blessings that Allah, exalted be His remembrance, intended to bestow upon him, whenever they saw him. This is so that they may infer from this that reward from Allah the Almighty is of two types: deserved (استحقاق) and specific bestowal (اختصاص). And so that they would not belittle a weak person for his weakness, nor a poor person for his poverty, nor a sick person for his sickness. And so that they may know that He causes sickness to whom He wills, when He wills, how He wills, and with whatever He wills, and He makes that an admonition (عبرة) for whom He wills, wretchedness (شقاوة) for whom He wills, and happiness (سعادة) for whom He wills. And He, the Mighty and Majestic, in all of that, is just in His decree and wise in His actions. He does not do anything to His servants except what is most beneficial (الأصلح) for them. And there is no power except with Allah.

And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the affliction of Ayyub (peace be upon him) with which He afflicted him in this world, for what reason was it? He said: It was due to a blessing with which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, favored him in this world, and he rendered thanks for it. At that time, Iblis was not veiled from the Throne (العرش). When he ascended and saw Ayyub's (peace be upon him) rendering of thanks for the blessing, Iblis envied him and said: "O Lord, Ayyub only rendered thanks for this blessing to You because of what You gave him of worldly possessions (الدنيا). If You deprived him of his worldly possessions, he would never render thanks for the blessing to You. So, grant me authority over his worldly possessions so that You may know that he will never render thanks for the blessing to You." It was said to him: "I have granted you authority over his wealth and his children." He said: So Iblis descended and did not leave him any wealth or children without destroying it. But Ayyub increased in thanks and praise to Allah. He (Iblis) said: "Grant me authority over his crops." He (Allah) said: "I have done so." So he gathered his devils and blew into it, and it burned up. But Ayyub (peace be upon him) increased in thanks and praise to Allah. He said: "O Lord, then grant me authority over his sheep." So He granted him authority over his sheep, and he destroyed them. But Ayyub (peace be upon him) increased in thanks and praise to Allah. He said: "O Lord, grant me authority over his body." So He granted him authority over his body, except for his intellect (عقله) and his eyes. Iblis blew into him, and he became one single ulcer (قرحة واحدة) from the crown of his head to his foot. He remained in that state for a long time, praising and thanking Allah, until worms fell into his body. They would emerge from his body, and he would return them, saying to them: "Return to your place from which Allah created you." And he became foul-smelling (نتن) until the people of the village expelled him from the village and threw him onto the garbage heap (المزبلة) outside the village. His wife, Rahmah, daughter of Yusuf ibn Ya'qub ibn Ishaq ibn Ibrahim (peace be upon them), would seek charity (تتصدّق) from people and bring him whatever she found. He said: When the affliction prolonged, and Iblis saw his patience, he came to Ayyub's (peace be upon him) companions, who were monks (رهباناً) in the mountains, and said to them: "Let us pass by this afflicted servant and ask him about his trial." So they rode gray mules (بغالا شهباء) and came. When they drew near him, their mules shied away from the foulness of his smell. They looked at one another, then walked to him. Among them was a young man of tender age. They sat down with him and said: "O Ayyub, if you would inform us of your sin, perhaps Allah would empower us when we ask Him. We do not see your affliction with this trial, with which no one has been afflicted, except due to a matter you were concealing." Ayyub said: "By the might of my Lord, He knows that I never ate food except that an orphan or a weak person ate with me, and never were two matters presented to me, both of which were obedience to Allah, except that I chose the harsher of the two upon my body." The young man said: "Shame upon you! You reproached the Prophet of Allah until he revealed some of the worship of his Lord that he used to conceal." Ayyub said: "O Lord, if I were to sit in the seat of judgment (مجلس الحكم) before You, I would present my argument." Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent a cloud to him, and He said: "O Ayyub, present your argument! I have seated you in the seat of judgment, and here I am near, and I have always been." He said: "O Lord, You know that never were two matters presented to me, both of which were obedience to You, except that I chose the harsher of the two upon myself. Did I not praise You? Did I not thank You? Did I not glorify You?" He said: Then a caller announced from the cloud with ten thousand tongues: "O Ayyub, who made you worship Allah while people were heedless of Him, and praise Him, glorify Him, and magnify Him while people were heedless of Him? Do you presume to place a favor upon Allah when the favor is His upon you?" He said: So he took dust and placed it in his mouth, then said: "Yours is the blame (العُتبى), O Lord. You did that for me." Then Allah sent down an angel to him, who kicked the ground with his leg, and water gushed forth. He washed himself with that water and returned to the best and freshest state he had been in. Allah caused a green meadow (روضة خضراء) to grow around him, and He restored to him His family, His wealth, His children, and His crops. The angel sat with him, speaking to him and comforting him. His wife approached, carrying a piece of bread (الكسرة). When she reached the place, behold, the place was changed, and two men were sitting there. She wept and cried out, saying: "O Ayyub, what has happened to you?" Ayyub called out to her, and she approached. When she saw him, and Allah had restored his body and his blessing, she prostrated to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, in thanks. He saw that her forelock (ذوابتها) was cut off. That was because she had asked some people to give her food to carry to Ayyub, and she had beautiful forelocks. They said to her: "Sell us this forelock of yours, and we will give you (food)." So she cut it off and gave it to them, and took food for Ayyub from them. When he saw her hair cut, he became angry and swore that he would strike her a hundred times. She informed him that the reason was such and such. Ayyub became distressed by this, so Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to him: خُذْ بِيَدِكَ ضِغْثاً فَاضْرِبْ بِهِ وَلا تَحْنَثْ ** (And take in your hand a bundle of rushes, and strike with it, and do not break your oath). So he took a cluster (عِذقاً) containing a hundred stalks (شمراخ) and struck her a single blow, thereby fulfilling his oath. He said: Then Allah restored to him his family who had died before the trial, and He restored to him his family who had died after the trial afflicted them; He brought them all back to life for him, and they lived with him. Ayyub (peace be upon him) was asked after Allah cured him: "What was the hardest thing you endured from what passed over you?" He said: "The gloating of enemies (شماتة الأعداء)." He said: Then Allah rained down upon his house golden locusts (جراد الذهب), and he began gathering them. When the wind carried some of them away, he ran after it to retrieve it. Jibril said to him: "Are you not satisfied, O Ayyub?" He said: "And who is satisfied with the provision of his Lord, the Mighty and Majestic?"

I say: Perhaps the meaning of his body (بدنه) mentioned in the first narration—that its scent did not become foul and it did not become infested with worms—is his original body (بدنه الأصلي) which is raised from the Prophets and Successors (الأوصياء) to the heaven, created from the clay (طينته) from which the souls of the believers were created. And the meaning of his body mentioned in this narration—that it became foul-smelling and infested with worms—is his elemental body (بدنه العنصريّ), which is like a sheath (كالغلاف) for the former, and the select ones (الخواص) do not care about it. Thus, there is no contradiction (تنافي) between the two narrations.

footnote (١) *Al-Middah* (*المدّة*) with a *kasrah* and the doubling of the undotted letter (*تشديد المهملة*): The thick pus that collects in a wound.

** وَاذْكُرْ عِبادَنا إِبْراهِيمَ وَإِسْحاقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ أُولِي الْأَيْدِي وَالْأَبْصارِ (45) And remember Our servants, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, men of strength and insight. al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام, who said: "They are those possessing strength (القوّة) in worship (العبادة) and insight (البصر) concerning it."

** إِنَّا أَخْلَصْناهُمْ بِخالِصَةٍ ** (46) Indeed, We purified them with a pure quality: We made them pure for Us by means of a pure quality (خصلة خالصة) in which there is no impurity (لا شوب فيها). This quality is the remembrance of the Abode (ذِكْرَى الدَّارِ), meaning their constant remembrance of the Hereafter (الآخرة). For their sincerity (خلوصهم) in obedience (الطاعة) is due to it. This is because their ultimate goal (مطمح نظرهم) in what they did and what they left was the proximity (جوار) of Allah and achieving success through meeting Him (الفوز بلقائه). The absolute use of the term 'the Abode' (الدار) is to indicate that it is the true Abode (الدار الحقيقية) and that the world (الدنيا) is merely a crossing point (معبر).

(٤٧) وَإِنَّهُمْ عِنْدَنا لَمِنَ الْمُصْطَفَيْنَ الْأَخْيارِ. (47) And indeed they are, in Our sight, among the chosen and the best. (٤٨) وَاذْكُرْ إِسْماعِيلَ وَالْيَسَعَ قيل هو ابن أخطوب استخلفه إلياس على بني إسرائيل ثمّ استنبأ وَذَا الْكِفْلِ هو يوشع بن نون كما مرّ في سورة الأنبياء وَكُلٌّ مِنَ الْأَخْيارِ. (48) And remember Ishmael, Elisha, It was said (قيل) that he is the son of Akhtub (ابن أخطوب). Elias (إلياس) appointed him as successor over the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل), and then he was made a prophet (استنبأ). and Dhul Kifl. He is Joshua son of Nun (يوشع بن نون), as passed in Surat al-Anbiya (سورة الأنبياء). And all of them are among the best. (٤٩) هذا ذِكْرٌ وَإِنَّ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ لَحُسْنَ مَآبٍ مرجع. (49) This is a reminder, and indeed for the Godwary there is a good destination: destination (مرجع). (٥٠) جَنَّاتِ عَدْنٍ مُفَتَّحَةً لَهُمُ الْأَبْوابُ. (50) Gardens of Eden, whose gates are open to them. (٥١) مُتَّكِئِينَ فِيها يَدْعُونَ فِيها بِفاكِهَةٍ كَثِيرَةٍ وَشَرابٍ قيل الاقتصار على الفاكهة للاشعار بأنّ مطاعمهم لمحض التلذّذ فانّ التغذّي للتحلّل ولا تحلّل ثمّة. (51) Reclining therein, they will call therein for abundant fruit and drink. It was said (قيل) that the restriction to fruit (الفاكهة) is to indicate that their foods are purely for pleasure (لمحض التلذّذ), because nourishment (التغذّي) is for decomposition (التحلّل), and there is no decomposition there (ثمّة). (٥٢) وَعِنْدَهُمْ قاصِراتُ الطَّرْفِ لا ينظرن إلى غير أزواجهنّ أَتْرابٌ لدات بعضهنّ لبعض لا عجوز فيهنّ ولا صبيّة. (52) And with them will be maidens of restrained glances, They do not look at anyone other than their husbands (لا ينظرن إلى غير أزواجهنّ), of equal age. peers (لدات) to one another; there is neither an old woman (عجوز) nor a young girl (صبيّة) among them. (٥٣) هذا ما تُوعَدُونَ لِيَوْمِ الْحِسابِ لأجله وقرئ بالياء. (53) This is what you are promised for the Day of Reckoning. for its sake (لأجله). And it was recited with the ya (بالياء). (٥٤) إِنَّ هذا لَرِزْقُنا ما لَهُ مِنْ نَفادٍ انقطاع. (54) This is indeed Our provision, which will never be exhausted. cessation (انقطاع). (٥٥) هذا الأمر هذا وَإِنَّ لِلطَّاغِينَ لَشَرَّ مَآبٍ. (55) This [is the case]. But indeed for the rebellious there is an evil destination: This (هذا) [is the matter (الأمر)] this. (٥٦) جَهَنَّمَ يَصْلَوْنَها فَبِئْسَ الْمِهادُ القمّيّ وهم الأوّل والثاني وبنو أميّة. (56) hell, which they shall enter, and what a terrible resting place! Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) [said]: They are the First (الأوّل) and the Second (الثاني) and Banu Umayyah (بنو أميّة). (٥٧) هذا فَلْيَذُوقُوهُ حَمِيمٌ وَغَسَّاقٌ وقرئ بالتخفيف هو ما يغسق اي يسيل من صديد أهل النار. والقمّيّ قال الغسّاق واد في جهنّم فيه ثلاثمائة وثلاثون قصراً في كلّ قصر ثلاثمائة بيت في كلّ بيت أربعون زاوية في كلّ زاوية شجاع في كلّ شجاع ثلاثمائة وثلاثون عقرباً في حمة كلّ عقرب ثلاثمائة وثلاثون قلّة من سمّ لو أنّ عقرباً نضحت سمّها على أهل جهنّم لوسعهم سمّها. (57) This [is the case]. So let them taste it: boiling water and pus, And it was recited with takhfif (بالتخفيف). It is what flows (يغسق), meaning, what runs (يسيل) from the pus (صديد) of the inhabitants of the Fire (أهل النار). And Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: Al-Ghassaq (الغسّاق) is a valley (واد) in Hell (جهنّم) containing three hundred and thirty palaces (قصراً). In every palace, there are three hundred houses (بيت). In every house, there are forty corners (زاوية). In every corner, there is a serpent (شجاع). In every serpent, there are three hundred and thirty scorpions (عقرباً). In the sting (حمة) of every scorpion, there are three hundred and thirty jars (قلّة) of poison (سمّ). If one scorpion were to spray its poison (نضحت سمّها) upon the inhabitants of Hell (أهل جهنّم), its poison would suffice them (لوسعهم سمّها).

(٥٨) وَآخَرُ مِن شَكْلِهِ أَزْوَاجٌ ** (and other pairs of the same kind.) And it was read (وقرئ) واخَر (وَأُخَر) in the plural (على الجَمع). مِنْ شَكْلِهِ ** It was said (قيل): [It means] from the like of what is tasted (مثل المذوق), or the punishment in severity (العذاب في الشدّة), or the like of the taster (مثل الذائق). أَزْواجٌ ** [It means] kinds (أصناف). And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) [said]: They are Banu al-Abbas (بنو العبّاس). (٥٩) هَذَا فَوْجٌ مُّقْتَحِمٌ مَّعَكُمْ لَا مَرْحَبًا بِهِمْ إِنَّهُمْ صَالُوا النَّارِ ** (‘This is a company rushing with you. No welcome for them! They will indeed enter the Fire.’) This is a narration (حكاية) of what will be said to the leaders of the transgressors (رؤساء الطاغين) when they enter the Fire, and a company (فوج) who followed them in misguidance (الضلال) enters with them. And al-iqtiham (الاقتحام) is undertaking hardship (ركوب الشدّة) and entering into it (والدخول فيها). In al-Majma' (المجمع) and al-Qummi (القمّيّ), narrated from the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم): "Indeed, the Fire will constrict upon them like the constriction of the spearhead (الزجّ) in the spear (بالرمح)." لَا مَرْحَبًا بِهِمْ ** This is an invocation (دعاء) from the followed ones (المتبوعين) against their followers (أتباعهم). إِنَّهُمْ صَالُوا النَّارِ Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) [said]: So Banu Umayyah (بنو أميّة) will say: لَا مَرْحَبًا بِهِمْ.**

(٦٠) ** قالُوا ** (60) They will say: meaning the followers to the leaders. بَلْ أَنْتُمْ لا مَرْحَباً بِكُمْ ** (Nay, you—no welcome for you!). Rather, you are more deserving of what you said, due to your misguidance and your leading others astray. أَنْتُمْ قَدَّمْتُمُوهُ لَنا ** (You brought it upon us). In al-Qummi, it is narrated that Banu Fulan will say: بَلْ أَنْتُمْ لا مَرْحَباً بِكُمْ أَنْتُمْ قَدَّمْتُمُوهُ لَنا ** (Nay, you—no welcome for you! You brought it upon us), (meaning that) you began with the oppression of the Family of Muhammad (prayers of Allah be upon them). فَبِئْسَ الْقَرارُ** (So, how evil is the abode!). So, how evil is the dwelling place: Hell.

(٦١) قالُوا ** (61) They will say: al-Qummi Then Banu Umayyah will say: رَبَّنا مَنْ قَدَّمَ لَنا هذا فَزِدْهُ عَذاباً ضِعْفاً فِي النَّارِ ** "Our Lord! Whoever prepared this for us, increase His punishment twofold in the Fire!" And that is that You increase upon his punishment its like, so it becomes two portions (ضِعْفَيْن) of the punishment. He said: They mean the First and the Second.

(٦٢) وَقالُوا ما لَنا لا نَرى رِجالاً كُنَّا نَعُدُّهُمْ مِنَ الْأَشْرارِ ** (62) And they will say, "What is the matter with us that we do not see men whom we used to count among the wicked? al-Qummi Then the enemies of the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) will say in the Fire: ما لَنا لا نَرى رِجالاً كُنَّا نَعُدُّهُمْ مِنَ الْأَشْرارِ ** "What is the matter with us that we do not see men whom we used to count among the wicked?" in the world, and they are the followers (شِيعَة) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

(٦٣) ** أَتَّخَذْناهُمْ سِخْرِيًّا ** (Did we take them in ridicule) هزواً (ridicule) is another description (صفة اخرى) for رجالاً (men). And it was read (وقرئ) with a ḍammah (بالضمّ) [i.e., سُخْرِيًّا], and with the interrogative hamzah (همزة الاستفهام) [in أَتَّخَذْناهُمْ], indicating a denial (إنكار) directed at themselves and a reprimand (تأنيب) for them regarding their mockery (الاستسخار) of them. أَمْ زاغَتْ عَنْهُمُ الْأَبْصارُ ** (or have the sights swerved from them?) [Meaning] they swerved (مالت) so we do not see them (فلا نريهم). And أَمْ (am) is equivalent to (معادلة ل) ما لَنا لا نَرى (Why do we not see?), indicating that the intended meaning is the negation of seeing them due to their absence (لغيبتهم), as if they said: "They are not here (ليسوا هاهنا)." أَمْ زاغَتْ عَنْهُمُ ** [means] our sights (أبصارنا).

(٦٤)** إِنَّ ذلِكَ لَحَقٌّ تَخاصُمُ أَهْلِ النَّارِ ** (64) Indeed, that is a certainty: the disputation of the inhabitants of the Fire. among themselves.

Al-Qummi: And that is the saying of al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Indeed, you are in Paradise rejoicing (تُحْبَرُونَ), while in the Fire you are sought." And it is added in al-Basa'ir: "so you are not found."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "Allah has indeed mentioned you when He recounted the saying of your enemy in the Fire: وَقالُوا ما لَنا لا نَرى [the rest of] the Ayah." He said: "By Allah, Allah did not mean or intend anyone other than you by this. You have become, in the eyes of the people of this world, among the worst of people, yet you, by Allah, are in Paradise rejoicing (تُحْبَرُونَ) and are sought in the Fire."

And in a narration: "By Allah, two of you shall not enter the Fire—no, by Allah, not even one! By Allah, you are those concerning whom Allah the Almighty said: **وَقالُوا ما لَنا ** [the rest of] the Ayah." Then he said: "By Allah, they sought you in the Fire, but they did not find anyone among you."

And in another [narration]: "When the inhabitants of the Fire settle in the Fire, they will look for you but will not see anyone among you. So, some of them will say to others: ما لَنا ** [the rest of] the Ayah." He said: "And that is the saying of Allah the Almighty: إِنَّ ذلِكَ لَحَقٌّ تَخاصُمُ أَهْلِ النَّارِ ** (Indeed, that is a certainty: the disputation of the inhabitants of the Fire). They will dispute concerning you, just as they used to speak [about you] in the world."

And in al-Majma' and al-Jawami': what is close to it.

(٦٥) ** قُلْ إِنَّما أَنَا مُنْذِرٌ وَما مِنْ إِلهٍ إِلَّا اللهُ الْواحِدُ الْقَهَّارُ ** (65) Say: 'I am only a warner, and there is no god except Allah, the One, the All-dominant, Say (O Muhammad) to the polytheists: I am only a warner (I warn you of Allah's punishment) and there is no god except Allah, the One, (Who has no partner) and is not divisible: the All-dominant (over everything).

(٦٦) ** رَبُّ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَما بَيْنَهُمَا الْعَزِيزُ الْغَفَّارُ ** (66) Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, the All-mighty, the All-forgiving.' Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them (He created them from Him, and to Him is their command) the All-mighty, (Who is not overcome when He punishes) the All-forgiving. (Who forgives whatever sins He wills for whomever He wills). And in these descriptions is an affirmation of monotheism (التوحيد), and a promise and a threat (وعيد) for the monotheists (الموحّدين) and the polytheists (المشركين), and a repetition of that which conveys the threat (الوعيد) and its precedence, because the claim (المدّعى) is the warning (الانذار).

(٦٧) قُلْ هُوَ نَبَأٌ عَظِيمٌ (67) Say, 'It is a great tiding, (٦٨) أَنْتُمْ عَنْهُ مُعْرِضُونَ ** (68) from which you are turning away.' It was said that is, what He informed you of. And it was said: what follows it, concerning the tiding of Adam. And القمّيّ (said) it means the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). And in البصائر, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "By Allah, it is the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him)." And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "The tiding (النبأ) is the Imamate (الإِمامة)."

(٦٩) ما كانَ لِي مِنْ عِلْمٍ بِالْمَلَإِ الْأَعْلى إِذْ يَخْتَصِمُونَ ** (69) I had no knowledge of the higher assembly when they contended. Since gaining knowledge of the speech of the angels and their mutual discourse is not achieved except through revelation. (٧٠) إِنْ يُوحى إِلَيَّ إِلَّا أَنَّما أَنَا نَذِيرٌ مُبِينٌ ** (70) All that is revealed to me is that I am only a manifest warner. Meaning: except because I am only... And it was recited انّما with a kasrah (بالكسر) based on narration (الحكاية).

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in the tradition of the Mi'raj (Ascension)—the beginning of which has already passed in the beginning of Surat Bani Isra'il (peace be upon him)—he said:

When He (the Prophet) reached Sidrat al-Muntaha (the Lote Tree of the Utmost Boundary), Gabriel stayed behind Him. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "O Gabriel, do you forsake me in this place?" He replied: "Go forward, ahead of you. By Allah, you have reached a station that none of Allah's creation before you has reached." So I saw the Light of my Lord, and the Subhah (السّبحة) intervened between me and Him. The Imam (peace be upon him) was asked: "What is the Subhah (السّبحة)?" So He gestured with His face toward the earth and with His hand toward the sky, saying: "The Majesty (جلال) of my Lord," three times. He (Allah) said: "O Muhammad!" I said: "Here I am, O Lord!" He said: "About what did the higher assembly (الملأ الأعلى) contend?" I said: "Glory be to You! I have no knowledge except what You have taught me." He said: So He placed His Hand—meaning the Hand of Power (يد القدرة)—between my shoulders, and I felt its coolness between my breasts. He said: He did not ask me about anything past or anything remaining except that I knew it. Then He said: "O Muhammad, about what did the higher assembly (الملأ الأعلى) contend?" I said: "About the expiations (الكفّارات), the ranks (الدرجات), and the good deeds (الحسنات)." Then He said to me: "O Muhammad, your sustenance has ceased, and your prophethood has concluded. Who is your successor (وصيّك)?" I said: "O Lord, I have tested Your creation, and I have not seen anyone among Your creation more obedient to me than Ali." He said: "And to Me, O Muhammad?" I said: "O Lord, I have tested Your creation, and I have not seen anyone among Your creation who loves me more intensely than Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him)." He said: "And to Me, O Muhammad?" Then give him the glad tidings that he is the banner of guidance (راية الهدى), the Imam of My friends (أوليائي), a light for whoever obeys Me, and the Word (الكلمة) which I have made incumbent upon the pious (المتّقين). Whoever loves him has loved Me, and whoever hates him has hated Me, in addition to the fact that I shall distinguish him with what I have not distinguished anyone else. So I said: "O Lord, my brother, my companion, my minister (وزيري), and my heir (وارثي)." He said: "This is a matter that has already been decreed: he shall be tested, and he shall be tested through others, in addition to the fact that I have bestowed upon him, and bestowed upon him, and bestowed upon him, and bestowed upon him four things." He clenched them with His Hand, but He did not elaborate on what He clenched.

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, he said: My Lord said to me: "Do you know about what the higher assembly (الملأ الأعلى) contends?" I said: "No." He said: "They contend regarding the expiations (الكفّارات) and the ranks (الدّرجات)." As for the expiations (الكفّارات), they are performing wudu' (الوضوء) completely during cold times (السبرات), moving the feet toward the congregational prayers (الجماعات), and awaiting the prayer after the prayer. And as for the ranks (الدرجات), they are spreading the greeting of peace (إفشاء السلام), feeding the hungry (إطعام الطعام), and praying at night while people are asleep.

And in al-Khisal: in another narration similar to it.

(٧١) إِذْ قالَ رَبُّكَ لِلْمَلائِكَةِ إِنِّي خالِقٌ بَشَراً مِنْ طِينٍ. (71) When your Lord said to the angels, 'Indeed I am going to create a human being from clay.' (٧٢) فَإِذا سَوَّيْتُهُ ** I made his creation proportionate, وَنَفَخْتُ فِيهِ مِنْ رُوحِي ** and I brought him to life by blowing the Spirit (الرّوح) into him, and [by] attributing it to Himself due to its nobility and purity. فَقَعُوا لَهُ ساجِدِينَ ** (72) When I have proportioned him and breathed into him of My Spirit, fall down before him in prostration.' So they fell down لَهُ ساجِدِينَ ** as an honor and veneration for him. The discussion concerning this has already passed in Surat al-Baqarah.

(٧٣) فَسَجَدَ الْمَلائِكَةُ كُلُّهُمْ أَجْمَعُونَ. (73) So the angels prostrated, all of them together, (٧٤) إِلَّا إِبْلِيسَ اسْتَكْبَرَ He acted arrogantly (تعظّم) وَكانَ مِنَ الْكافِرِينَ and was one of the faithless in Allah's knowledge. (٧٥) قالَ يا إِبْلِيسُ ما مَنَعَكَ أَنْ تَسْجُدَ لِما خَلَقْتُ بِيَدَيَ. (75) He said, 'O Iblis! What prevented you from prostrating before that which I have created with My own hands? In al-Uyun and al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him), he said: "It means by My power (قدرتي) and My strength (قوّتي)." And al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "If Allah Almighty had created all creation with His hand (بيده), He would not have needed to specify regarding the creation of Adam that He created him with His hand, saying: ما مَنَعَكَ أَنْ تَسْجُدَ لِما خَلَقْتُ بِيَدَيَ ** (What prevented you from prostrating before that which I have created with My own hands?). Do you think Allah sends forth things with His hand (بيده)? أَسْتَكْبَرْتَ أَمْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الْعالِينَ ** (Did you act arrogantly, or are you one of the exalted ones?). Did you become arrogant without deserving it, or were you among those who are exalted (علا) and deserved superiority (التفوّق)?"

(٧٦) قالَ أَنَا خَيْرٌ مِنْهُ خَلَقْتَنِي مِنْ نارٍ وَخَلَقْتَهُ مِنْ طِينٍ ** (76) He said, 'I am better than him: You created me from fire, and You created him from clay.' Its explanation has passed in Surat al-A'raf. (٧٧) قالَ فَاخْرُجْ مِنْها فَإِنَّكَ رَجِيمٌ. (77) He said, 'Then get out of it, for you are indeed an outcast. (٧٨) وَإِنَّ عَلَيْكَ لَعْنَتِي إِلى يَوْمِ الدِّينِ. (78) And My curse shall be on you until the Day of Retribution.' (٧٩) قالَ رَبِّ فَأَنْظِرْنِي إِلى يَوْمِ يُبْعَثُونَ. (79) He said, 'My Lord! Respite me till the day they are resurrected.' (٨٠) قالَ فَإِنَّكَ مِنَ الْمُنْظَرِينَ. (80) He said, 'You are indeed among the reprieved (٨١) إِلى يَوْمِ الْوَقْتِ الْمَعْلُومِ ** (81) until the day of the known time.' Its explanation has passed in Surat al-Hijr. (٨٢) قالَ فَبِعِزَّتِكَ ** Meaning: By Your sovereignty (سلطانك) and Your subjugation (قهرك) لَأُغْوِيَنَّهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ. (82) He said, 'Then by Your might, I will surely mislead them all (٨٣) إِلَّا عِبادَكَ مِنْهُمُ الْمُخْلَصِينَ ** (83) except Your exclusive servants among them.' Those whom Allah has made sincere (أخلصهم) or those who have made their hearts sincere (أخلصوا قلوبهم) for Allah, based on the difference in the two readings (القراءتين). (٨٤) قالَ فَالْحَقُّ وَالْحَقَّ أَقُولُ ** (84) He said, 'The truth is, and the truth I speak: Meaning: I establish the truth (فاحقّ الحقّ) and I speak it. And al-Qummi (said): So Allah said: The truth (الحقّ), meaning: You shall indeed do that, and the truth I speak. And it was read with the raising (رفع) of the first word (الْحَقُّ) as a subject (الابتداء), meaning: The truth (الْحَقُّ) is My oath (يميني), or as a predicate (الخبر), meaning: I am the Truth (أنا الْحَقُّ). (٨٥) لَأَمْلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنْكَ وَمِمَّنْ تَبِعَكَ مِنْهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ. (85) I will surely fill hell with you and with all those who follow you among them.' (٨٦) قُلْ ما أَسْئَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ ** Upon the conveyance (of the message) (على التبليغ) وَما أَنَا مِنَ الْمُتَكَلِّفِينَ ** (86) Say, 'I do not ask you any reward for it, and I am not one of the impostors.' Those who pretend (المتصنّعين).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He (peace be upon him) said to the enemies of Allah, the allies of Satan, the people of denial (التكذيب) and rejection (الإنكار): ** قُلْ ما أَسْئَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ وَما أَنَا مِنَ الْمُتَكَلِّفِينَ ** (Say, 'I do not ask you any reward for it, and I am not one of the impostors.') He (the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) says, pretending (متكلّفاً), that I ask you for what you are not worthy of. Thereupon, the hypocrites (المنافقون) said to one another: "Is it not enough for Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that he has subjugated us for twenty years, until he now wants to place his Household (أهل بيته) upon our necks?" They said: "Allah did not reveal this, and it is nothing but something he fabricates, desiring to elevate his Household (أهل بيته) upon our necks. And if Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is killed or dies, we shall surely strip it (authority) from his Household, and we shall never restore it to them."

In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Rida, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon them both): The Muslims said to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "O Messenger of Allah, if you were to compel those among the people whom you have power over to embrace Islam, our numbers would increase, and we would be strong against our enemy." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "I would not meet Allah, the Exalted, with an innovation (بدعة) concerning which He has not revealed anything to me. ** وَما أَنَا مِنَ الْمُتَكَلِّفِينَ.** (and I am not one of the impostors.)"

In al-Jawami', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, he said: "The impostor (المتكلّف) has three signs (علامات): he disputes with those above him, he attempts what he cannot attain, and he says what he does not know."

In al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), from Luqman: The like of it.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): "And among the scholars (العلماء) are those who put themselves forward for religious rulings (الفتاوى) and say, 'Ask me,' yet perhaps he does not get a single letter correct. And Allah does not love ** the impostors ** (الْمُتَكَلِّفِينَ). Such a person is in the sixth level (الدّرك السّادس) of the Fire."

In Misbah al-Shari'ah, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "The impostor (المتكلّف) is mistaken, even if he happens to be correct. The impostor does not attract in the outcome of his affair anything but humiliation (الهوان), and in the present time, nothing but fatigue (التعب), hardship (العناء), and misery (الشقاء). The impostor's outward (ظاهره) and inward (باطنه) are hypocrisy (نفاق), and these are two wings with which the impostor flies. Generally, imposition (التكلّف) in any matter is not among the morals (اخلاق) of the righteous (الصالحين) nor among the characteristics (اشعار) of the pious (المتّقين)." The Almighty Allah said to His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: ** قُلْ ما أَسْئَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ وَما أَنَا مِنَ الْمُتَكَلِّفِينَ.** (Say, 'I do not ask you any reward for it, and I am not one of the impostors.')

(٨٧) إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكْرٌ ** (Admonition) لِلْعالَمِينَ. (87) It is nothing but a reminder for all the nations. (٨٨) وَلَتَعْلَمُنَّ نَبَأَهُ ** (concerning the promise and the threat) بَعْدَ حِينٍ. (88) and you will surely know its tidings after a while. In al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), He said: [This will be] upon the emergence of the Qā’im (peace be upon him). In Thawāb al-A‘māl and al-Majma‘, narrated from al-Ayyāshī, from al-Bāqir (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Sūrat Ṣād on the night of Friday will be granted of the good of this world and the Hereafter what has not been granted to anyone among mankind, except for an entrusted Prophet (نبيّ مرسل) or a proximate angel (ملك مقرّب), and Allah will admit him into Paradise, along with everyone he loves from his household (أهل بيته), even his servant who serves him, even if he was not within the limit of his dependents (عياله) nor within the limit of those for whom he may intercede.
